items.title: White House Issues Executive Order on Advanced AI
items.summary: The administration issued an executive order addressing artificial intelligence innovation and national security risks.
items.details: On June 2, 2026, the White House issued an order titled 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security.' It directs federal agencies to strengthen cyber defenses and outlines voluntary frameworks for the secure development of frontier AI models.

items.title: President Signs Secure America Act Into Law
items.summary: The Secure America Act, funding the Department of Homeland Security through FY2029, was sent to the president after House passage.
items.details: The reconciliation measure provides funding to DHS, Customs and Border Protection, and Immigration and Customs Enforcement through September 2029 for immigration enforcement, border security technology, and related activities. The House passed it June 9 following Senate passage June 5.

items.title: Israel Strikes Hezbollah Command Center in Beirut
items.summary: Israel said it struck a Hezbollah command center in Beirut hours after Hezbollah launched drones at northern Israel.
items.details: According to Israeli and U.S. officials, the Israeli military notified U.S. Central Command shortly before the Beirut strike. President Trump urged Israel and Hezbollah to exercise restraint, and the escalation came hours before the U.S. and Iran were expected to sign a ceasefire agreement.
items.source: Just Security (citing The New York Times)

items.title: Russia Disputes Account of Overnight Strike in Kyiv
items.summary: Russia said it did not strike a historic monastery and cathedral site in Kyiv during an overnight attack.
items.details: Moscow stated that a U.S.-made Patriot air defense missile had damaged the religious site and that its attack had targeted drone manufacturing facilities. The strikes followed a call between Presidents Trump and Putin.

items.title: House Passes Secure America Act 214-Vote Margin
items.summary: The House passed S. 2, the Secure America Act, ending a standoff over Department of Homeland Security funding.
items.details: The House cleared the bill on June 9, with 214 Republicans in favor and Democrats and the chamber's lone independent opposed. The measure funds DHS, ICE, and CBP through September 2029 and now goes to the president's desk.

items.title: House Passes DOMINANCE Act on Critical Minerals
items.summary: The House passed bipartisan legislation related to overseas mineral investments and allied networks for critical energies.
items.details: On June 8, the U.S. House passed H.R. 7037, the Developing Overseas Mineral Investments and New Allied Networks for Critical Energies (DOMINANCE) Act, introduced by Representatives Ami Bera and Young Kim.

items.title: FISA Section 702 Authorization Lapses
items.summary: Congress's authorization for FISA's Section 702 surveillance program lapsed amid a week of legislative activity.
items.details: According to a GovTrack legislative recap, several anti-fraud bills passed mostly by voice vote and DHS received additional funding through 2029, while the Section 702 authorization lapsed, though the program itself is likely to continue uninterrupted.

items.title: Supreme Court Allows Alabama to Use Disputed Map
items.summary: The Supreme Court cleared the way for Alabama to use a congressional map that lower courts found racially discriminatory.
items.details: In an unsigned June 2 order, the court divided 6-3 and stayed a lower-court ruling that had barred Alabama's 2023 map, citing the Purcell principle and its April decision in Louisiana v. Callais. Justices Sotomayor, Kagan, and Jackson dissented.

items.title: Supreme Court Decides Venue in Falsification Case
items.summary: The court ruled on where a defendant charged under a federal record-falsification statute must be tried.
items.details: In Abouammo v. United States, decided June 11, the court held that a defendant charged with violating 18 U.S.C. §1519 must be tried in the district where the falsification occurred rather than where the investigation was located.

items.title: Alabama Map Ruling Reshapes 2026 Districts
items.summary: After the Supreme Court order, Alabama's 2026 midterm map features six Republican-leaning districts and one Democratic-leaning district.
items.details: The reinstated 2023 map replaces a court-ordered map with two districts where Black voters had a realistic chance to elect their preferred candidate. The change affects the seat held by Democratic Rep. Shomari Figures in the 2nd District.

items.title: Republicans Largely Prevail in Redistricting Battle
items.summary: Reporting found Republicans gained ground in the nationwide redistricting fight ahead of the 2026 midterms.
items.details: Following a U.S. Supreme Court decision narrowing the Voting Rights Act, several Southern states moved to redraw maps. South Carolina's Senate rejected a redraw, while Missouri's top court upheld a Republican-drawn map.

CA.title: Newsom Signs Election Security Bill SB 73
CA.summary: Governor Newsom signed Senate Bill 73, restricting unauthorized law enforcement access to polling places and ballots.
CA.details: Signed May 27 with an urgency clause taking effect immediately, the law limits when law enforcement can access ballots, voter lists, and certified voting technology. It followed the seizure of certified ballots by the Riverside County Sheriff during the Proposition 50 special election.

FL.title: DeSantis Signs Additional Batches of Bills
FL.summary: Governor DeSantis signed dozens more bills into law in mid-June, many addressing local and county matters.
FL.details: Across mid-June, DeSantis signed multiple batches of legislation, adding to over 160 laws approved in 2026. Several take effect July 1, while some, including local appropriations measures, took effect immediately upon signing.

NY.title: Hochul Signs World Cup Bar Hours Extension
NY.summary: Governor Hochul signed legislation temporarily extending bar and restaurant hours during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
NY.details: The new law allows on-premises licensees to sell alcoholic beverages until 4 a.m. from June 11 through July 20, 2026. It takes effect immediately and is repealed on July 21, 2026.

PA.title: Shapiro Presents 2026-27 Budget Proposal
PA.summary: Governor Shapiro proposed a $53.2 billion budget for the 2026-2027 fiscal year ahead of the June 30 deadline.
PA.details: The proposal marks a $2.7 billion increase, about 5.4% over the prior fiscal year, including increases for human services and education. Senate Republicans said they would work to reduce spending in a more fiscally restrained plan.

OH.title: DeWine Signs Bills Including Parole Recording Law
OH.summary: Governor DeWine signed several bills into law in mid-June, including measures on parole hearings and park discounts.
OH.details: On June 12, DeWine signed bills including HB 31, requiring electronic recordings of certain parole board hearings and making full hearing recordings public records, and SB 223, establishing a discount program for veterans and service members for state park services.

CO.title: Polis Signs Homeowners Insurance Cost Bill
CO.summary: Governor Polis signed legislation supporters say is aimed at lowering homeowners insurance costs in Colorado.
CO.details: Signed June 5, SB26-155 (Increase Access Homeowner's Insurance Enterprise) is designed to make hail-resistant roofs more affordable, decrease premiums, and increase market competition in high-wildfire areas, as part of efforts the governor says could save residents on annual costs.

MO.title: Missouri Supreme Court Upholds GOP-Drawn Map
MO.summary: The Missouri Supreme Court upheld a Republican-drawn congressional map for use in the 2026 elections.
MO.details: In rulings handed down May 12, the court found the map drawn in a 2025 special session is constitutional and may be used in the August primary, and that a referendum petition's submission did not automatically suspend the map. The map is designed to net Republicans an additional House seat.

SC.title: South Carolina Senate Rejects Redistricting Push
SC.summary: The Republican-led state Senate rejected a measure to take up a redraw of the state's congressional map.
SC.details: A resolution to extend the session for redistricting fell short of the two-thirds support needed, with five Republicans voting against. The vote ended, for now, an effort to redraw the state's lone majority-Black district held by Rep. James Clyburn before the midterms.

LA.title: Louisiana Lawmakers Pass New Congressional Map
LA.summary: The Louisiana legislature passed a new congressional map that eliminates one of the state's two majority-Black districts.
LA.details: Passed in late May after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down the prior map, the new plan favors Republicans in five districts and Democrats in one. It reshapes District 6, currently held by Democratic Rep. Cleo Fields.

VA.title: Virginia Redistricting Effort Ends After Court Rulings
VA.summary: A U.S. Supreme Court decision ended efforts to revive Virginia's voter-approved redistricting referendum.
VA.details: The Supreme Court of Virginia struck down the amendment on May 8, and the U.S. Supreme Court later rejected Democrats' emergency appeal, leaving in place the congressional districts adopted in 2021. Several Democratic candidates ended campaigns launched under the proposed map.

GA.title: Georgia Election Board Advances Hand-Marked Ballot Resolution
GA.summary: Georgia's State Election Board passed a nonbinding resolution seeking hand-marked paper ballots for the November 2026 general election.
GA.details: Passed June 4 in a 3-1 vote, the resolution contradicts guidance from the secretary of state's office directing counties to continue using current voting machines. The state's elections director said existing law does not permit a different voting or tabulation method.

AL.title: Alabama to Use 2023 Map After Supreme Court Order
AL.summary: Alabama will use its 2023 congressional map in 2026 after the U.S. Supreme Court stayed a lower-court ruling.
AL.details: State Attorney General Steve Marshall had filed an emergency application after a three-judge panel found the 2023 map intentionally racially discriminatory. The Supreme Court's June 2 order allowed the state to proceed with the map, and a special primary is set for August 11.

TX.title: Abbott Opens 2026 Star of Texas Award Nominations
TX.summary: Governor Abbott announced that nominations for the 2026 Star of Texas Awards are open.
TX.details: The awards honor peace officers, firefighters, emergency medical first responders, and federal law enforcement personnel who were seriously injured or killed in the line of duty. Nominations are due to the Office of the Governor by June 15, 2026.

disclaimer: This digest reports verifiable developments from cited sources for civic-education purposes and does not endorse any party, candidate, or position.
